role_summary: Civil rights movement participant; co-subject of a joint oral history with Raylawni G. Branch (catalog entry 100).
ai_reading: Jeanette Smith shares the embedding signature of the joint oral history at entry 100 (catalog slug 'raylawni-g-branch-and-jeanette-smith'); see that catalog page for the substrate-derived neighbor cluster and axis observations the embedding produces for the combined transcript. Individual cosines for each named subject of a joint interview are not separable from the precomputed substrate, since the LoC transcript was recorded as a single audio file with multiple voices interleaved.
biographical_paragraph: Jeanette Smith is one of the named subjects of the joint Civil Rights History Project oral history at entry 100 (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. This catalog page exists as a per-person index for Jeanette Smith; the substrate signature lives on the parent joint-interview page (see the AI's reading above) since the LoC transcript treats the joint conversation as a single recording. Jeanette Smith's Civil Rights History Project oral history is cross-referenced against the Library of Congress published transcript.

sources.title: Joint oral history at entry 100
sources.publisher: Civil Rights History Project, Library of Congress (AFC 2010/039)

gallery.alt: College Hall, University of Southern Mississippi, Hattiesburg
gallery.caption: College Hall at the University of Southern Mississippi in Hattiesburg, the city where Jeanette Smith organized for voting rights and education with the NAACP and COFO, and where her co-interviewee Raylawni Branch helped integrate the university in 1965.
